---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
infect <- read.table(file = "infect.txt", header = FALSE, sep = "\t", quote = "", dec = ".", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

#barplot( infect$V2, horiz = TRUE, main = "bar plot of infectivity ratio", xlab = "Nef+/Nef- infectivity ratio", names.arg = infect$V3)
 

cell_colors <- c(
  "Jurkat tag"  = "steelblue",
  "JURKAT E6.1" = "steelblue",
  "bl41"      = "seagreen3",
  "RAMOS"     = "seagreen3",
  "CEM A 301" = "seagreen3",
  "CEM SS"     = "seagreen3",
  "HSB2"     = "seagreen3",
  "RAJI"      = "red3",
  "C8166"     = "red3",
  "WI38"      = "red3",
  "DAUDI"     = "red3",
  "MT4"       = "red3",
  "IMR90"     = "red3",
  "CEM X 174"   = "red3",
  "HT1080"    = "red3"
)


#clean_name <- function(x) {
#  toupper(gsub("[^A-Z0-9]", "", x))
#}

#infect$V3_clean <- clean_name(infect$V3)
names(cell_colors) <- clean_name(names(cell_colors))

infect$color <- "grey60"

idx <- infect$V3 %in% names(cell_colors)
infect$color[idx] <- cell_colors[infect$V3[idx]]

infect_sorted <- infect[order(infect$V2), ]

par(mar = c(4, 8, 2, 1))

barplot(
  infect_sorted$V2,
  horiz = TRUE,
  names.arg = infect_sorted$V3,
  las = 1,
  col = infect_sorted$color,
  border = NA,
  xlab = "Nef+/Nef- infectivity ratio",
  xlim = c(0, 40)
)


```

```{r}

############################################
## COMPLETE PIPELINE: SERINC5 vs Infectivity
############################################

## -------- SETTINGS --------
gene_id <- "ENSG00000164300"   # SERINC5
expr_folder <- "D:/sem8/biostatistics/transcriptome-data-analysis/gene data"

## -------- STEP 1: READ INFECTIVITY DATA --------
#infect <- read.table(
#  "infect.txt",
#  header = FALSE,
#  sep = "\t",
#  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
#)

colnames(infect) <- c("SRR", "Infectivity", "CellLine")
infect$Infectivity <- as.numeric(infect$Infectivity)

## -------- STEP 2: MAP SRR IDs TO CELL LINES --------
file_to_cellline <- c(
  "SRR2166626" = "HT1080",
  "SRR2166634" = "CEM X 174",
  "SRR2166632" = "IMR90",
  "SRR2166624" = "MT4",
  "SRR2166629" = "DAUDI",
  "SRR2166635" = "WI38",
  "SRR2166630" = "C8166",
  "SRR2166627" = "RAJI",
  "SRR2166625" = "HSB2",
  "SRR2166628" = "CEM SS",
  "SRR2166633" = "CEM A 301",
  "SRR2166631" = "RAMOS",
  "SRR2166637" = "bl41",
  "SRR2166636" = "JURKAT E6.1",
  "SRR2166638" = "Jurkat tag"
)

## -------- STEP 3: READ & MERGE EXPRESSION FILES --------
files <- list.files(expr_folder, full.names = TRUE)

read_expr <- function(file) {
  df <- read.table(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  df <- df[, 1:2]

  srr <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file))
  cellline <- file_to_cellline[srr]

  colnames(df) <- c("GeneID", cellline)
  df
}

expr_list <- lapply(files, read_expr)

expr_merged <- Reduce(
  function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "GeneID", all = TRUE),
  expr_list
)

## -------- STEP 4: EXTRACT SERINC5 --------
serinc5 <- expr_merged[expr_merged$GeneID == gene_id, -1]

serinc5 <- as.data.frame(t(serinc5))
serinc5$CellLine <- rownames(serinc5)
rownames(serinc5) <- NULL

colnames(serinc5) <- c("Expression", "CellLine")
serinc5$Expression <- as.numeric(serinc5$Expression)

## -------- STEP 5: MERGE WITH INFECTIVITY --------
data <- merge(infect, serinc5, by = "CellLine")
data <- data[complete.cases(data[, c("Infectivity", "Expression")]), ]

## sanity check (should be 15)
print(nrow(data))

## -------- STEP 6: COLOR MAPPING --------

cell_colors <- c(
  "Jurkat tag"  = "steelblue",
  "JURKAT E6.1" = "steelblue",
  "bl41"      = "seagreen3",
  "RAMOS"     = "seagreen3",
  "CEM A 301" = "seagreen3",
  "CEM SS"    = "seagreen3",
  "HSB2"      = "seagreen3",
  "RAJI"      = "red3",
  "C8166"     = "red3",
  "WI38"      = "red3",
  "DAUDI"     = "red3",
  "MT4"       = "red3",
  "IMR90"     = "red3",
  "CEM X 174" = "red3",
  "HT1080"    = "red3"
)
data$color <- cell_colors[data$CellLine]

## -------- STEP 7: CORRELATION --------
ct <- cor.test(data$Infectivity, data$Expression, method = "pearson")
r  <- round(ct$estimate, 3)
r2 <- round(r^2, 4)

## -------- STEP 8: SCATTER PLOT --------
par(mar = c(4, 5, 2, 1))

plot(
  data$Infectivity,
  data$Expression,
  pch = 21,
  bg  = data$color,
  col = "black",
  cex = 1.7,
  xlab = expression("Nef"^"+"*"/Nef"^"-"*" infectivity ratio"),
  ylab = "SERINC5 expression (RPM)"
)

abline(lm(Expression ~ Infectivity, data = data), lwd = 2)

text(
  x = min(data$Infectivity),
  y = max(data$Expression),
  labels = bquote(
    atop(
      italic(r) == .(r),
      atop(
        italic(R)^2 == .(r2),
        italic(P) < 0.001
      )
    )
  ),
  adj = c(0, 1),
  cex = 1
)

```

```{r}

############################################
## GENE-WISE CORRELATION WITH INFECTIVITY
############################################

## -------- SETTINGS --------
expr_folder <- "D:/sem8/biostatistics/gene data"

## -------- STEP 1: READ INFECTIVITY DATA --------
infect <- read.table(
  "infect.txt",
  header = FALSE,
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

colnames(infect) <- c("SRR", "Infectivity", "CellLine")
infect$Infectivity <- as.numeric(infect$Infectivity)

## -------- STEP 2: SRR → CELL LINE MAP --------
file_to_cellline <- c(
  "SRR2166626" = "HT1080",
  "SRR2166634" = "CEM X 174",
  "SRR2166632" = "IMR90",
  "SRR2166624" = "MT4",
  "SRR2166629" = "DAUDI",
  "SRR2166635" = "WI38",
  "SRR2166630" = "C8166",
  "SRR2166627" = "RAJI",
  "SRR2166625" = "HSB2",
  "SRR2166628" = "CEM SS",
  "SRR2166633" = "CEM A 301",
  "SRR2166631" = "RAMOS",
  "SRR2166637" = "bl41",
  "SRR2166636" = "JURKAT E6.1",
  "SRR2166638" = "Jurkat tag"
)

## -------- STEP 3: READ & MERGE EXPRESSION FILES --------
files <- list.files(expr_folder, full.names = TRUE)

read_expr <- function(file) {
  df <- read.table(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  df <- df[, 1:2]

  srr <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file))
  cellline <- file_to_cellline[srr]

  colnames(df) <- c("GeneID", cellline)
  df
}

expr_list <- lapply(files, read_expr)

expr_merged <- Reduce(
  function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "GeneID", all = TRUE),
  expr_list
)

## -------- STEP 4: REORDER EXPRESSION COLUMNS TO MATCH INFECTIVITY --------
cell_order <- infect$CellLine
expr_mat <- expr_merged[, cell_order]
expr_mat <- apply(expr_mat, 2, as.numeric)

## -------- STEP 5: COMPUTE GENE-WISE CORRELATIONS --------
cor_results <- apply(expr_mat, 1, function(gene_expr) {

  valid <- is.finite(gene_expr) & is.finite(infect$Infectivity)

  if (sum(valid) < 3) {
    return(c(r = NA, R2 = NA, p = NA))
  }

  ct <- cor.test(
    gene_expr[valid],
    infect$Infectivity[valid],
    method = "pearson"
  )

  c(
    r  = unname(ct$estimate),
    R2 = unname(ct$estimate)^2,
    p  = ct$p.value
  )
})

## -------- STEP 6: FINAL RESULTS TABLE --------
cor_df <- data.frame(
  GeneID = expr_merged$GeneID,
  t(cor_results),
  row.names = NULL
)

cor_df$r  <- as.numeric(cor_df$r)
cor_df$R2 <- as.numeric(cor_df$R2)
cor_df$p  <- as.numeric(cor_df$p)

## -------- STEP 7: MULTIPLE TEST CORRECTION --------
cor_df$FDR <- p.adjust(cor_df$p, method = "BH")

## -------- STEP 8: SORT BY EFFECT SIZE --------
cor_df <- cor_df[order(-abs(cor_df$r)), ]

## DONE
head(cor_df)


```
